name: Montañas Vacías: Javalambre Peak Loop
name_original: Ascensión a Javalambre — circular desde Camarena de la Sierra
description: This difficult 29.4-mile mountain bike route to Pico Javalambre offers challenging climbs, panoramic views, and unique high-altitude landsca
region_intro: Mountain biking around Camarena De La Sierra offers diverse landscapes within the Sierra de Javalambre mountain range. The region is characterized by high-mountain scenery, including the Javalambre Peak, and extensive natural trails. Abundant springs and riverine paths create lush environments, while forests and fields provide varied terrain for mountain bikers. The area features a mix of challenging climbs and accessible paths, catering to different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Camarena De La Sierra The most popular mountain bike trail is Montañas Vacías: Javalambre Peak Loop, a 29.4 miles (47.2 km) difficult trail that takes 4 hours 53 minutes to complete. This route ascends towards the prominent Javalambre Peak, offering significant elevation gain and panoramic views. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Teruel – Teruel Cathedral Square loop from Puebla de Valverde, a moderate 36.1 miles (58.1 km) path. This route explores the varied terrain around nearby towns, featuring a mix of open fields and forest sections. Local mountain bikers also love the View of Riodeva – Amanaderos Old Trail loop from Riodeva, a 17.6 miles (28.3 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes and forests, often completed in about 3 hours 9 minutes. Mountain biking around Camarena De La Sierra is defined by high-mountain scenery, extensive forests, and riverine paths. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Camarena De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 150 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Camarena De La Sierra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Camarena De La Sierra?
A: There are over 20 mountain bike trails around Camarena De La Sierra, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when mountain biking in Camarena De La Sierra?
A: You can expect diverse landscapes, ranging from characteristic high-mountain scenery in the Sierra de Javalambre to extensive natural trails, fields, forests, and attractive riverine paths. The area offers varied terrain suitable for different mountain biking styles, including sections that might require pushing your bike on steeper climbs.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for mountain bike trails in this area?
A: The trails in Camarena De La Sierra primarily cater to moderate and difficult skill levels. There are 9 moderate routes and 11 difficult routes available, ensuring options for experienced riders seeking a challenge.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Camarena De La Sierra?
A: Given its location in the Sierra de Javalambre mountain range and the presence of a ski resort, the warmer months from late spring through early autumn are generally the best for mountain biking. Winters can be cold and snowy, making many trails inaccessible.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls or significant water features?
A: Yes, the region is known for its abundant springs and the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4457070" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amanaderos Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;. You can explore trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31429450" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Riodeva – Amanaderos Old Trail loop from Riodeva&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through riverine landscapes and forests, offering picturesque views of these natural attractions.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints accessible by mountain bike in the area?
A: Mountain bikers can reach significant natural landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3925573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Javalambre&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic views. Another prominent point is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4457133" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de las Barracas&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features historical sites like El Castillejo, an ancient Islamic fortress and Christian medieval castle.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Camarena De La Sierra?
A: While many trails are rated moderate to difficult, the region's riverine paths and extensive fields offer less technical sections that might be suitable for families with some biking experience. However, specific easy-rated trails are not prominent in the current selection. It's advisable to check individual route descriptions for suitability.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Camarena De La Sierra?
A: The mountain bike routes in Camarena De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Riders often praise the diverse high-mountain scenery, the extensive forests, and the engaging riverine paths that define the area's unique riding experience.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996627284/montanas-vacias-javalambre-peak-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montañas Vacías: Javalambre Peak Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31902762" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Teruel – Teruel Cathedral Square loop from Puebla de Valverde&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the mountain bike trails in Camarena De La Sierra?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on mountain bike trails are not detailed, generally, dogs are permitted on many natural trails in Spain, especially in rural mountain areas. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs under control, especially around wildlife or livestock, and to check local signage for any specific restrictions.

Q: Are there any facilities for bike rentals or repairs in Camarena De La Sierra?
A: While the immediate Camarena De La Sierra area is more focused on natural beauty, nearby regions like Matarraña offer services such as bike transport, rentals, and repairs. It's advisable to check with local tourism offices or specialized bike shops in larger nearby towns for current availability.

Q: What are some of the longer mountain bike routes in the region?
A: For those seeking longer rides, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31902762" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Teruel – Teruel Cathedral Square loop from Puebla de Valverde&lt;/a&gt; cover approximately 36.1 miles (58.1 km). Another substantial option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14709341" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Amanaderos of Riodeva – View of Riodeva loop from Riodeva&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 34.2 miles (55 km) long.
tips: This refugio at 1,720 metres altitude on the Sierra de Javalambre is basic, but the roof is completely new and the interior has been restored. It has a special charm.
The summit is 2019 meters high. The path is demanding, but it is worth the effort. The panoramic views of the surrounding mountains are like something out of a movie.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Ascensión a Javalambre mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult. It involves significant elevation changes and demands considerable physical effort, with continuous climbing to reach the summit of Pico Javalambre. While not overly technical, the sustained ascents, especially the initial demanding gradients, require good physical condition.

faqs.question: What is the estimated duration for completing this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for completing this challenging 47.25 km mountain bike route is approximately 4 hours and 53 minutes. However, this can vary significantly based on your fitness level, the number of stops, and weather conditions.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: You will encounter a diverse range of terrain, including well-maintained forest tracks, some secondary paved roads, and occasional singletrack sections. Expect gravel ascents, particularly as you climb towards Pico Javalambre, and some 'false flats' that can be deceptively demanding.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Highlights include the Collado del Buey Shelter and the summit of Pico Javalambre, which offers spectacular panoramic views. Near the top, you'll also pass by the Javalambre Astrophysical Observatory, and experience unique 'lunar' and 'leopard skin' landscapes formed by creeping savins.

faqs.question: Where is the starting point for this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This circular route starts and ends in Camarena de la Sierra. Parking is generally available within the village, though specific trailhead parking might require local inquiry.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this trail is typically from late spring through early autumn. During winter, the high-altitude sections, especially near Pico Javalambre, can be covered in snow, potentially requiring mountaineering equipment like crampons and an ice axe.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required for this route, especially within the Parque Natural de la Puebla de San Miguel?
faqs.answer: While parts of the route may pass near or through areas associated with the Parque Natural de la Puebla de San Miguel, there are generally no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking on established trails like this one. However, always respect local regulations and signage, especially concerning protected areas.

faqs.question: Can I bring my dog on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: Information regarding dogs on this specific route is not explicitly stated. However, as a general rule in natural areas in Spain, dogs are often permitted but must be kept under strict control, usually on a leash, to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always check local signage for any specific restrictions.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several established trails. Notably, it follows parts of the MontañasVacías (39% of the route), the Ruta del Parque Natural de la Puebla de San Miguel (22%), and the De Valencia a Lisboa (Etapa Camarena de la Sierra - Riodeva) (21%). It also uses sections of the GR-8 and GR-10.

faqs.question: Is there access to water along the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail research mentions passing by fountains. However, it's always recommended to carry sufficient water for the entire duration of this challenging ride, as reliable water sources can be scarce or seasonal. Do not rely solely on natural sources without proper filtration.

faqs.question: What essential gear should I bring for this ride?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, essential gear includes a well-maintained mountain bike, a helmet, sufficient water and snacks, appropriate clothing layers for changing mountain weather, a repair kit, and a first-aid kit. GPS navigation is highly recommended as signage can be scarce and confusing in some areas.
